bounties.name: SQL Server
bounties.tagline: Finish read/write support and production-grade SQL Server workflows.
bounties.description: The driver is already in flight on a feature branch. Phase 2 needs help on TLS options, composite primary keys, FK aggregation and write support.
bounties.nextStep: Claim one of the open Phase 2 issues and target the SQL Server feature branch.
bounties.signal: Phase 1 done. Phase 2 is where contributors can move the release.

bounties.name: Amazon Redshift
bounties.tagline: Validate Redshift as a first-class analytical warehouse workflow.
bounties.description: Redshift speaks a PostgreSQL-derived dialect, but full support still needs warehouse-specific validation for metadata, pagination, auth, explain output, and query behavior.
bounties.nextStep: Build a Redshift test matrix around connection auth, schema discovery, query execution, exports, and docs.
bounties.signal: Good candidate for a compatibility bounty because it can reuse parts of the PostgreSQL path only after real warehouse testing.

bounties.name: CockroachDB
bounties.tagline: Make distributed SQL feel native instead of merely connectable.
bounties.description: CockroachDB uses the PostgreSQL wire protocol, but full support needs validation around schemas, introspection, transactions, explain output, and docs.
bounties.nextStep: Create a CockroachDB fixture and document which PostgreSQL flows work unchanged and which need dialect handling.
bounties.signal: Likely starts as compatibility validation, not a separate driver.

bounties.name: MariaDB
bounties.tagline: MariaDB is covered by the built-in MySQL/MariaDB workflow.
bounties.description: The site already positions MariaDB support as part of the core database set. The bounty opportunity is documentation, compatibility coverage, and MariaDB-specific polish.
bounties.nextStep: Expand MariaDB-specific docs and edge-case tests around metadata, types, and version differences.
bounties.signal: Already supported through the core MySQL/MariaDB driver; future work should focus on confidence and docs.

bounties.name: TiDB
bounties.tagline: Validate TiDB compatibility without pretending MySQL support is enough.
bounties.description: TiDB speaks the MySQL protocol, but distributed SQL behavior, metadata, transactions, and explain output need targeted validation before claiming full support.
bounties.nextStep: Run the MySQL/MariaDB workflow against TiDB and document gaps before deciding if a dialect layer is needed.
bounties.signal: A compatibility bounty can turn protocol support into a real support claim.

bounties.name: Oracle
bounties.tagline: A serious Oracle workflow without forcing users into heavyweight tooling.
bounties.description: Connection profiles, schema browsing, query execution, routines and export support for teams that still live around Oracle databases.
bounties.nextStep: Open a technical proposal covering driver approach, wallet support, and licensing constraints.
bounties.signal: Highest impact after SQL Server for enterprise users evaluating Tabularis.

bounties.name: Cassandra
bounties.tagline: Expose keyspaces, tables, CQL queries, and practical inspection workflows.
bounties.description: Cassandra support should be scoped around CQL query execution, keyspace/table discovery, paging, and safe read workflows before attempting broad administration features.
bounties.nextStep: Define a minimal CQL driver contract and identify whether ScyllaDB can share the same implementation.
bounties.signal: Worth treating as the base driver for ScyllaDB compatibility too.

bounties.name: ScyllaDB
bounties.tagline: Use the Cassandra path as the base, then validate Scylla-specific behavior.
bounties.description: ScyllaDB can likely ride on a Cassandra/CQL driver, but full support should still document compatibility and any metadata or paging differences.
bounties.nextStep: Scope ScyllaDB as a validation layer on top of the Cassandra bounty.
bounties.signal: Best bundled with Cassandra so the board does not create two duplicate driver efforts.

bounties.name: Firebird
bounties.tagline: Bring a durable embedded/server SQL database into the plugin ecosystem.
bounties.description: Firebird support needs connection setup, metadata inspection, query execution, type handling, and docs for both local and server deployments.
bounties.nextStep: Choose the driver/runtime approach and define the first read-only feature set.
bounties.signal: Strong fit for the plugin model because it expands SQL coverage without changing the core binary.

bounties.name: LibSQL
bounties.tagline: Extend SQLite workflows toward LibSQL and remote Turso-style databases.
bounties.description: LibSQL sits close to SQLite but adds remote workflows and sync-oriented assumptions. It should be validated as a separate compatibility target.
bounties.nextStep: Define whether LibSQL should extend the SQLite driver or ship as a plugin with remote auth support.
bounties.signal: Useful for developers who already use SQLite locally but deploy on LibSQL/Turso-like infrastructure.

bounties.name: SQL Anywhere
bounties.tagline: Support long-lived enterprise and embedded SQL Anywhere installations.
bounties.description: SQL Anywhere is niche but valuable for teams maintaining existing systems. The first target should be reliable read-only access and schema inspection.
bounties.nextStep: Find a maintainer with SQL Anywhere access and define the driver dependency strategy.
bounties.signal: Good bounty candidate if a real user can provide access, test cases, and review feedback.

bounties.name: Trino / Presto
bounties.tagline: Connect Tabularis notebooks to federated SQL engines.
bounties.description: Trino and Presto support should focus on catalogs, schemas, query execution, result paging, and docs for federated analytics workflows.
bounties.nextStep: Scope catalog/schema discovery and test against Trino first, then decide Presto compatibility.
bounties.signal: Pairs well with SQL notebooks because the value is federated analysis, not table editing.

bounties.name: SurrealDB
bounties.tagline: Explore a focused SurrealQL workflow inside Tabularis.
bounties.description: SurrealDB needs a deliberately scoped plugin: connection, namespaces/databases, SurrealQL execution, result inspection, and docs before broad multi-model UI work.
bounties.nextStep: Define the smallest useful SurrealQL workflow and document what should not be part of v1.
bounties.signal: Interesting ecosystem signal, but should stay query-first until the product shape is clear.

bounties.name: DynamoDB
bounties.tagline: Inspect tables, keys, indexes, and targeted reads without building an AWS console clone.
bounties.description: DynamoDB support should be scoped around table discovery, key/index inspection, safe item reads, and AWS credential handling.
bounties.nextStep: Write the first technical brief around AWS auth, table metadata, query/scan safety, and local DynamoDB testing.
bounties.signal: A good future target, but only if the first version avoids dangerous broad scans by default.

bounties.name: MongoDB
bounties.tagline: Bridge document collections into a focused desktop workflow.
bounties.description: A plugin for browsing collections, inspecting document shape, running filtered reads and exporting result sets without turning Tabularis into a generic NoSQL IDE.
bounties.nextStep: Define the first useful read-only workflow before adding write operations.
bounties.signal: Strong community pull, but scope must stay narrow and maintainable.

bounties.name: Snowflake
bounties.tagline: Cloud warehouse access for analytics teams using Tabularis notebooks.
bounties.description: Connection setup, warehouse/database/schema switching, result pagination and notebook-first analytics workflows for Snowflake users.
bounties.nextStep: Validate auth, warehouse switching, pagination, and notebook fit before opening implementation issues.
bounties.signal: Best fit when paired with notebooks and repeatable reporting flows.

bounties.name: Google BigQuery
bounties.tagline: Run warehouse queries from the same workspace as local and app databases.
bounties.description: A BigQuery plugin would connect cloud analytics with Tabularis notebooks, query history, exports and AI-assisted SQL workflows.
bounties.nextStep: Shape OAuth, dataset discovery, query jobs, and cost guardrails into a first issue.
bounties.signal: High leverage for teams that mix app databases with analytics data.

bounties.name: Elasticsearch
bounties.tagline: Inspect indices, sample documents and run search queries from Tabularis.
bounties.description: A search-oriented plugin for index browsing, mappings, document samples and query execution without building a full observability platform.
bounties.nextStep: Limit the first version to inspection, mappings, document samples, and search queries.
bounties.signal: Useful if scoped around inspection and query workflows, not cluster ops.

bounties.name: Redis
bounties.tagline: Virtual table views for strings, hashes, lists, sets and sorted sets.
bounties.description: Redis support proves the plugin architecture can handle non-relational systems while still exposing a native Tabularis workflow.
bounties.nextStep: Use the shipped Redis plugin as a reference for future non-relational integrations.
bounties.signal: Shipped through the external plugin path.

bounties.name: DuckDB
bounties.tagline: Local analytical database support through the plugin ecosystem.
bounties.description: DuckDB gives Tabularis a fast path for local analytics, CSV-heavy workflows and embedded analytical workloads.
bounties.nextStep: Use the shipped DuckDB plugin as a reference for local analytical engines.
bounties.signal: Shipped as a standalone driver plugin.

bounties.name: ClickHouse
bounties.tagline: Columnar database workflows for fast analytical systems.
bounties.description: ClickHouse support extends Tabularis into high-volume analytical databases without bloating the core binary.
bounties.nextStep: Use the shipped ClickHouse plugin as a reference for OLAP database drivers.
bounties.signal: Shipped through the plugin registry.
